title: Multi-class share structure
summary: Enables founders to issue voting and non-voting share classes with liquidation preferences, replacing the rigid one-share-one-vote rule that forces African startups to incorporate offshore.
addresses: Tunisia Commercial Code Article 154 (one-share-one-vote)
Nigeria CAMA Section 124 (one-share-one-vote)
OHADA Uniform Act (2014) — no provisions for multi-class structures
Ethiopia Commercial Code — no multi-class authorisation

versions.label: OHADA / civil-law (Senegal, Ivory Coast, DRC, Tunisia)

title: Simple Agreement for Future Equity (SAFE)
summary: A standardised early-stage financing instrument adapted from the Y Combinator SAFE, with explicit recognition under labelled-startup regimes and OHADA-compatible drafting.
addresses: Tunisia Capital Market Decree 2018-1037 (no SAFE recognition)
Nigeria SEC Rules 2013 (classifies SAFEs as debt)
BCEAO Instruction 2012-09 (only recognises debt and equity)
OHADA Uniform Act on commercial companies (no recognition for hybrid instruments)
Ethiopia Securities Proclamation (debt and equity only)

versions.label: OHADA / civil-law (compatible with sociétés par actions)

title: Convertible Note
summary: A standardised convertible debt instrument that converts to equity on a qualified financing, change of control, or maturity. Pairs with the SAFE clause as the second early-stage financing primitive — easier to issue in OHADA jurisdictions (AUSCGIE Articles 822-839 provide a mature OCA framework) and in jurisdictions where the SAFE's hybrid classification is contested.
addresses: Tunisia BCT memo Feb 2020 (classifies SAFEs as debt; OCA is debt by nature, escaping the classification ambiguity)
Nigeria SEC Rules 2013 (treats SAFEs as debt; convertible notes fit cleanly within CAMA debenture framework)
BCEAO Instruction 2012-09 (recognises debt and equity only; OCA recognised as debt)
Senegal Loi n° 2020-01 Article 12 (mentions titres convertibles but lacks OHADA integration)
Ghana Securities Industry Act 2016 (Section 109 — debentures expressly permitted)
South Africa Companies Act 71/2008 Section 43 (debt issuance framework)
Tunisia Code des Sociétés Commerciales Articles 332-355 (mature obligations convertibles framework)

versions.label: Common-law (Nigeria anchor; Kenya, Ghana, South Africa parallel)
OHADA / civil-law (Senegal anchor; Ivory Coast, DRC, Tunisia parallel)

title: Employee Stock Option Plan (ESOP)
summary: A complete kit — Plan + Award Agreement + Notice of Exercise + Cap Table schedule — drafted natively for African company law to enable tax-at-liquidity equity compensation. Frames the ESOP as a labor reform: more employee-owners, knowledge work paid in equity, options that travel with the employee rather than locking them to an offshore parent.
addresses: Tunisia IRPP Article 38 (taxes equity grants as immediate wages on theoretical values)
Nigeria Labour Act + PIT Act (no equity-comp provisions, taxes spread at exercise)
Senegal Code du Travail Articles L.40 and L.67 (rigid employment structures)
DRC Code du Travail Articles 40 and 67
Ivory Coast SYSCOHADA Articles 10-15 (no clear treatment for stock options)
Ethiopia Labor + Income Tax Proclamation (no deferred-tax framework for equity)
Kenya Income Tax Act Section 5 (taxes options at exercise)
Ghana Income Tax Act Sections 4, 6 (taxes options at exercise)
South Africa Income Tax Act Section 8C (taxes at vesting, not liquidity)

versions.label: Common-law (Nigeria anchor; Kenya, Ghana, South Africa parallel)
OHADA / civil-law (Senegal anchor; Ivory Coast, DRC, Tunisia parallel)
